question_frequency.rationale: The Pair only presented one didactic choice point (the A/B options in turn 2), and only after the user explicitly demanded it. No follow-up questions probed the user's understanding before or after the choice.

question_quality.rationale: The single A/B choice hits the actual crux of this problem — the sequential-vs-atomic substitution gotcha when mappings cycle (G↔C). That's the textbook teachable moment for this exercise.

scaffolding.rationale: There is essentially one question with no progression. Worse, Option A's framing ('applies the entire mapping atomically in a single pass') arguably hints at the flaw in B by contrast, weakening the discovery aspect.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is warm and the Pair gracefully owned the missed setup ('You're right, my mistake'). But flow is poor: the Pair shipped a solution unprompted in turn 1, ignoring the entire didactic premise until corrected.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
